destinations.evidence: Port scheduler parsing carries caller from packed Scheduler state into ScheduledCall instead of defaulting to zero.
Generated TxScheduled values propagate call.caller into the scheduled transaction metadata.
destinations.snippet: TxScheduled::new_with_options(..., origin_hash, obligation.call_id, obligation.index, call.start_block as u64, call.frequency as u64, call.ttl as u64, call.caller, None, None, None)

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: the event-tracking surface is replaced by parent-state parsing, and the caller field is preserved through ScheduledCall into generated TxScheduled metadata.
notes: Mapped via parent-state parser, not old event logs.
rationale: The assigned candidate establishes that caller must not be defaulted to zero. Current referenced scheduler parsing code has no storage-read parser but decodes caller from each enriched event log and propagates enhanced_event.caller into tracked call state.
statement: Scheduler call parsing and tracking must carry the caller address decoded from scheduler call data into in-memory call/event state instead of defaulting caller to zero. Current local evidence obtains caller from enriched event logs rather than from storage slot 5.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create Scheduler storage with a non-zero caller and assert read_scheduled_call returns that caller and synthesized TxScheduled carries it.
summary: Scheduler parsing preserves caller in the port replacement path.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Scheduled call storage parsing and TxScheduled synthesis should preserve a non-zero caller.
title: Scheduler call parsing preserves caller address
